title: There are 4 box sets on Amazon which should I get
display_title: There are 4 box sets on Amazon which should I get
wikitext:
[]: Box Set             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 2:49 pm So I ordered "Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Complete-DVD" from Best Buy. But I was wondering, since it didn't have an image of it on the site, which version did I order?

[]: Trust me... I'm your dearest friend.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:20 pm According to the product page it's not even in stock, and it says it'll ship "when available." I'm not sure which set this is, but if they don't have it in stock now then they'll never have it. Cancel your order and buy this one . It's twice as much, but at least you'll actually get it and it's the best set out there, IMO. It comes with the entire series plus all of the bonus features that were on the original individual DVDs. I've ordered Evangelion stuff that's been "not in stock" or "backordered" before. Trust me, you'll never get it. If $80 is too rich for your blood, try to find the Complete Platinum Thin-pack on Ebay. It's the one I have, and the same as the one above except without the bonus features(i.e., just the episodes). It's the one they're selling on Best Buy for $57(backordered).

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:23 pm I got an email from them, and they said they had to back order it, and that it'll come in One to Two weeks. Plus I ordered it on a gift card, so I don't think I can cancel it.

[]: There is no email. Just like there is no spoon.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:25 pm soda7777777 wrote: I got an email from them, and they said they had to back order it, and that it'll come in One to Two weeks. Plus I ordered it on a gift card, so I don't think I can cancel it.

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:30 pm Alright then, I'll just order a copy off of amazon sometime. Did they refund your order when you did this?

[]:             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:33 pm soda7777777 wrote: Alright then, I'll just order a copy off of amazon sometime. Did they refund your order when you did this?

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:37 pm Killer Bee wrote: They won't charge you until the order ships, so if it doesn't ship you won't get charged. I'm not sure how gift cards work; you might have to call customer service about that.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:41 pm Pff you should go the distance and get the Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum: Perfect Collection, it's what I got, and it was worth every penny.

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 3:47 pm facespace wrote: Pff you should go the distance and get the Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum: Perfect Collection, it's what I got, and it was worth every penny.

[]: Re: Trust me... I'm your dearest friend.             Quote Post by kirri » Thu Jul 29, 2010 6:15 pm Killer Bee wrote: Cancel your order and buy this one . It's twice as much, but at least you'll actually get it and it's the best set out there, IMO. It comes with the entire series plus all of the bonus features that were on the original individual DVDs.

[]: Re: Box Set             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 29, 2010 6:25 pm soda7777777 wrote: So I ordered "Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum Complete-DVD" from Best Buy. But I was wondering, since it didn't have an image of it on the site, which version did I order?

[]: Re: Box Set             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 29, 2010 8:41 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: It's probably the "Thinpack"...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 29, 2010 9:40 pm I was just at Best Buy and they had the Holiday Set* with the movies, and it was on sale for only 40-something dollars. I was pissed, I bought my replacement for the original black box set from ebay for about $35 w/ shipping and it was only the episodes and some trailers, absolutely no extras. * Holiday Set: http://www.bestbuy.com/site/Neon+Genesis+Evangelion:+Complete+Platinum+Collection+[Holiday+Limited+Edition]+[7+Discs]+-+DVD/9072013.p?id=1927787&skuId=9072013&st=Evangelion%20holiday%20set

[]: There are 4 box sets on Amazon, which should I get??             Quote Post by LeCapEtan » Fri Jul 30, 2010 10:42 am Here are the four sets... -Neon Genesis Evangelion Platinum: Perfect Collection -Neon Genesis Evangelion - Perfect Collection -Neon Genesis Evangelion: Platinum Collection -Neon Genesis Evangelion: Complete Platinum Collection (Limited Edition Holiday Special) Money isn't an issue for this purchase, so which set do you guys recommend?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 30, 2010 7:53 pm Nix the Perfect Collection, it's the original box and has inferior image and sound quality, no director's cuts, and poorer subtitles. The three Platinum Collections you list are all six one way and half a dozen the other. I'm pretty sure they're all the same but just with different artwork. I prefer the slimcase set just because it's the most economical and because all of the extras on the other editions aren't worth it.

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Fri Jul 30, 2010 8:08 pm I went there today, and had to deal with customer service for almost two hours. They used a gift card, because I payed in cash when I had gone. But the employee had never given me the gift card. So now their sending me a gift card with the money in return. But I did cancel the order Also, they seemed to think that Best Buy does carry it, and that since it got a 100% review, that I would be getting it. But the person reviewing the item, was reviewing the Holiday Edition, and not the one that I ordered. Suffice to say, I'm never buying from Best Buy again.

[]:             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Fri Jul 30, 2010 8:28 pm Well I bought the Platinum Perfect Collection, but I don't know if you want to spend Almost 70$ on the Eva dvds in a pretty box.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 30, 2010 9:08 pm I have the Perfect Platinum (metal tin) edition. It has the slim packs and director's cuts like the platinum slim pack edition has, but it also has all of the extras. Yojimbo's right in that most of the extras are not worth it. But I do like the storyboards/animatics for the episodes that are provided, but they're not on every disc for every episode. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Jul 31, 2010 1:52 pm I have the "Limited Edition Holiday Special" boxset, I think that one is worth checking out.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sat Jul 31, 2010 2:10 pm Get the Perfect Platinum for the delicious storyboards, definitely.
